title: IBKR Portfolio Report v2

tradeTape.note: Entire book built in a single ~6-minute window on 2026-06-22, 18:33–18:39 UTC — six BUYs. The −4.2% drawdown hit the very next session. Commissions (6 × ~$1) explain the −$5.98 cash balance.

etfLookThrough.concentrationNote: 100% Basic Materials · HHI 1,924 (~5.2 effective names) · BATT+LIT = 45.8% of NAV in two heavily-overlapping ETFs. Real diversification is lower than 6 lines suggests.

metrics.note: high market beta — moves ~1.7× the index
low vs the sector — behaves like a slice of XME
6 names, ~5.2 effective — high corr lowers real diversification

driversNote: Mixed-to-headwind backdrop: lithium & palladium are correcting and a firming dollar (DXY +1.8% YTD) pressures all USD commodities. Rare earths (Dy +105% YTD) are the standout tailwind — good for REMX. The Fed is on a hawkish hold (3.50–3.75%, ~68% odds of a Sept hike) given sticky 4.17% CPI, so the easing tailwind is on pause.

smartMoney.detail: Citigroup upgrade to Buy (PT $225, Jun 18); UBS/Citi PT hikes; mean PT ~$195→$225 over the quarter. Offset: CCO sold 5,692 sh ($942k) Feb 20.
Tianqi (21.9% holder) exited its Series B stake; controlling blocks (Pampa 25.8%, Tianqi 21.9%) steady; Berenberg Hold, flags valuation stretched.

options.note: ALB ATM implied vol ≈ 59% (Aug expiry, ~58 DTE) — the options market prices a large forward move, consistent with 62% realized vol. Mild downside skew: the 10%-OTM put trades ~1.1 vol points above the call (60.0% vs 58.9%).

performance.note: 60-day current-weights backtest: the book returned +0.5% vs SPY +15.7% and XME +4.7% — heavy underperformance, driven by ALB (−16%) and palladium (−11%). Positions have only been held since Jun 22; this is a current-weights backtest, not realized performance.

bottomLine: −4.1% since inception, essentially all from buying on Jun 22 immediately before a complex-wide sell-off. A concentrated, highly-correlated (0.67) single-theme bet: ~38% volatility, high market beta (1.69) but sector-like metals beta (0.69), ~17.6% hidden China exposure, and a dominant sensitivity to the lithium price (a −20% lithium move ≈ −$1,200). Lithium & palladium are correcting and the dollar is firming; rare earths are the bright spot. Watch: ALB earnings Jul 30, SQM Aug 19, the Sep FOMC, and the Nov 10 US–China truce expiry.
sources: IBKR (live account) · TipRanks · Bigdata.com · FRED/Federal Reserve · TrueNorth/FMP commodity feed · Carbon Arc · web. Risk metrics from 60 daily closes (2026-03-27→06-23). Informational only — not investment advice.
